hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., E193, F205), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding clashes with conserved residues in those subtypes. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 selectivity, but structural issues are limiting the number of viable candidates. Tightening structural constraints and increasing the candidate pool should improve the pass rate without compromising selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding pocket or receptor-ligand interface. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and improve structural quality.
A low number of candidates increases the risk of missing structurally valid designs. Increasing the candidate pool will improve chances of identifying high-quality structures.
